dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8329 IH: Form 990 Immigrant Resettlement Schedule Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Fischbach (for herself and Mr. Finstad) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to require the reporting of information on activities relating to the resettlement or relocation of noncitizens into the United States by certain tax-exempt organizations, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Form 990 Immigrant Resettlement Schedule Act.

section.header: Reporting of information on activities relating to resettlement or relocation of noncitizens into the United States by certain tax-exempt organizations

subsection.text: Section 6033(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking and at the end of paragraph (15)(B), by redesignating paragraph (16) as paragraph (17), and by inserting after paragraph (15) the following new paragraph:
such information ordinarily collected by the organization as the Secretary may require with respect to—

subparagraph.text: the aggregate number of individuals who—

clause.text: are not citizens of the United States, and
are assisted by the organization during the taxable year in any activities relating to the lawful resettlement or relocation of such individuals into the United States,

subparagraph.text: the aggregate number of individuals described in subparagraph (A) who are assisted by the organization during the taxable year in any activities relating to the enrollment or participation of such individuals in any Federal benefit program (as defined in section 552a(a)(12) of title 5, United States Code), as well as any cash or in-kind assistance received thereunder by such individuals, and
the direct or indirect nature of the organization’s assistance or involvement in the activities described in subparagraph (A)(ii) or (B), and

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following: For purposes of paragraph (16), the organization shall not be required to furnish any information which can be associated with, or otherwise identify, directly or indirectly, a particular individual described in subparagraph (A) thereof..

subsection.text: The amendments made by subsection (a) shall apply with respect to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2026.

Not later than December 31, 2028, and annually thereafter, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transmit to Congress an aggregate statistical report on any information described in section 6033(b)(16) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by subsection (a)) which was furnished to the Secretary thereunder during the preceding calendar year.
